This very serious incident occurred when the V1 struck a row of shops/buildings opposite the Town Hall in Acre Lane . One of the buildings was being used as a rest centre for people who had previously been forced out of their homes by Flying bombs. As well as the rest centre 10 houses and shops were demolished and 20 houses damaged. Lambeth Town Hall, a modern building, clearly stood up well to the blast and no structural damage can be detected. However careful inspection will reveal that the Windows frames down the side of the Acre Lane side of the town hall are in a slightly different style to elsewhere in the building and must have been completely blown out. On the other side of the road the sight of the rest centre and shops has been replaced with 1960's style new shops filling the gap.

Positioning: Amesbury Avenue Junction with Hillside Road
Description: The V1 struck at the junction of Amesbury Road and Hillside Road . 10 houses were totally demolished and a large number badly damaged. The area around the impact site has been extensively re-developed with post war housing and shops
The V1 struck between Baytree and Sudbourne Road . 20 houses were demolished and 40 very badly damaged. The area has been re-developed with post war flats.

Positioning: Streatham Hill Corner of Barhill Road

Description: The V1 hit The South side of Trelawn road.11 houses were demolished and 40 badly damaged in both Trelawn and Morval Roads. The gap in the line of housing can still be seen. In Morval road the in-filling in now being rebuilt for a second time since the war time demolition

Positioning: Brixton water Lane/ St Matthews Road
Description: The V1 struck at the junction of Water Lane And St Matthew Road . 10 houses were demolished and 30 damaged. The area today has been totally re-developed so it is not possible to ascertain the impact site
The V1 fell in Brockwell Park . No casualties were reported by the Fire Brigade an Open air Theatre and 50 houses were damaged by the blast.
The V1 demolished 4 houses on Tulse Hill in the area of the post war St Martins Estate A further 30 were badly damaged. No casualties were reported by the Fire Brigade.
The V1 divested the area around the Effra Parad/Dalberg Road junction. 12 houses were demolished and 40 badly damaged. The area today has been totally re-developed with flats.

Positioning: Calders place (no longer there ) which was at the apex of Holsmsdale Road
Description: The V1 struck Calders place. The blast badly damaged and industrial building, demolished 12 houses and badly damaged 40 others. No casualties are listed. The area has been totally re-developed post war.

The V1 badly damaged the girls school at the Wavertree Road/ Daysbrook Road junction. 10 house were demolished and 20 badly damaged in Daysbrook Road . 2 were demolished and 10 badly damaged in Wavertree Road . The damage area extended right down Daysbrook Road , including number 2 slightly damaged where I lived for 6 years from 1990. The school has been re-built and carries a plaque which commemorates the event. The demolished houses in Daysbrook Road have been replaced with 60's housing, further down the frontages of the remaining Victorian houses show signs of significant re-building.

The V1 injured 24 people. 3 houses were demolished and 30 badly damaged in Montrell Road . 3 were demolished and 20 damaged in Tierney Road . A further 3 were demolished and 20 damaged in Streatham Place . Today post war housing fills the site but the tell tale signs of blanked off gable ends of once terraces housing is clear to see.

21 people were injured by this V1 strike. It demolished 14 houses in Bellasis Avenue and badly damaged 50 in Bellasis Avenue , Criefell Avenue and Thornton Avenue . The road has been totally re-developed with 60's housing
V1 fell on Clapham Common near Mount Pond . No damage is reported
The V1 fell on the South side of Clapham Common. Some damage to surrounding property .

Positioning: Stockwell Park Road by Lorn Road
Description: V1 in Stockwell Park Road by L orn Road.
V1 fell on open ground on Clapham Common, some damage to surrounding properties.
The V1 fell in Ellerslie St . It is reported that "Back buildings" and 50 houses were damaged by blast. The site has been re-developed as an industrial estate
The V1 fell in Studley Road . 10 houses were demolished and 30 severely damaged.
One of 2 V1's that fell in this area. 12 houses were demolished in Kepler Road , 20 badly damaged in Kepler Road and 12 in Solon New Road . The area has been totally re-developed as a housing estate
1 of 2 V1's in Gauden Road . I cannot ascertain which date matches which location. One fell at the Station end, the other on the North side by Broomfield Road junction.
2nd of 2 V1's in Gauden Road . I cannot ascertain which date matches with location. One fell at the Station end, the other on the North side by Broomfield Road junction.
The V1 fell in Paradise Road and hit a gasometer at the South Metropolitan gas company
The 2nd of 2 V1's that fell in this area. Very little details were recorded. The area has been totally re-developed as a housing estate.
The V1 struck Downers cottages demolishing 20 houses and causing severe damage to 30 others.
The V1 hit Clapham Road opposite Clapham North Tube. 6 houses were demolished and 20 houses and a Church severely damaged. The area has been re-developed as a housing estate and the church re-built

Positioning: Lesser Avenue Position between Lessar and Cautley avenues half way up
Description: The V1 struck Lesser Avenue and demolished 20 houses and caused severe damage to 20 others. There was a small fire in the debris.

Positioning: Rumsey road between Benedict and Speenham Road
Description: The V1 struck in Rumsey Road . 12 houses were demolished and 40 damaged.

Positioning: Brixton Road Cambewell New Road junction SE Quad
Description: The V1 struck Brixton Road and Camberwell New Road . A range of buildings used as workrooms,office and stores suffered severe damage
The V1 demolished 11 houses and caused severe damage to 30 more. and area of 200 yards square was blast damaged.
The V1 fell in Cavendish Road and demolished 6 houses and caused severe damage to 20 others. . The area has been re-developed with a housing estate
V1 in Barrington Road . No casualties reported. 2 houses demolished and 40 damaged an ews brick and steel dam damaged by blast.

Positioning: Kings Avenue at Clarence road junction
Description: The V1 fell at the junction of Kings Avenue and Clarence Road . It demolished 12 flats and 40 houses and also caused much damage to Queenswood court. This building has tell tale metal window frames which were installed as replacements.

Positioning: Paradise Road Clarence walk junction SW quad
Description: The V1 fell in Paradise Road . Stockwell Methodist Church Hall was damaged.
V1 on open ground on Clapham Common. Slight damage to surrounding property.
The V1 hit Pathos Road and Lothian Street.8 houses were demolished and 30 badly damaged.
The second V1 to fall in Studley Road ... 10 houses demolished and 30 suffered severe damage.
The V1 hit Manor house road. 20 houses demolished and 30 damaged.

The V2 fell on Clapham Common near the Pavement. No casualties are reported but the following damage is listed : 30 houses and a factory damaged on North side 60 houses and shops damaged on South Side 65 houses and shops damaged in Clapham High st 20 houses and shops damaged in the Pavement

The V2 fell in Rodenhurst Road . No casualties were reported but the following damage is listed : 4 houses demolished in Rodenhurst rd 44 flats slight damage at Poynders court 65 flats slight damage at Poynders gdns 45 houses slight damage at Elms Crescent 8 houses severe damage Trouvill Road + 45 slight damage There is much post war development at this point including some unusual 2 storey pre-fabs
